UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the asset management system, excluding authentication details. This entity supports features like collaboration and user-specific settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firebaseUid.description: The unique ID provided by Firebase Authentication for the user. This links the UserProfile to the external authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, typically obtained from the authentication provider.

displayName.description: The user's display name or full name.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile record was last updated.

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Represents a single physical or virtual asset managed by the system, covering various types from different Excel sheets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

serialNumber.description: The unique serial number of the asset, often found on labels.

model.description: The model name or number of the asset.

location.description: The current physical location or assignment of the asset.

assetType.description: Categorization of the asset (e.g., 'Ntb far', 'Motorcycles', 'Vehicles'), derived from the source Excel sheet or manual entry.

acquisitionDate.description: The date when the asset was acquired.

currentValue.description: The current estimated monetary value of the asset.

status.description: The current operational status of the asset (e.g., 'Operational', 'Under Repair', 'Disposed').

ocrConfidenceScore.description: A confidence score (0-100) indicating the accuracy of data extracted by OCR for this asset.

ocrLastProcessedAt.description: Timestamp of the last time an OCR process was run for this asset's data.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initially created this asset record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Asset)

lastModifiedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who last modified this asset record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Asset)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the asset record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the asset record was last updated.

AssetFile.title: AssetFile
AssetFile.description: Represents a file (photo, document, attachment) associated with a specific asset, stored in Firebase Storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssetFile entity.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset this file belongs to. (Relationship: Asset 1:N AssetFile)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

storagePath.description: The path or URI where the file is stored in Firebase Storage.

fileSize.description: Size of the file in bytes.

isScannedDocument.description: Flag indicating if this file originated from the integrated document scanner.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this file. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AssetFile)

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

ConditionNote.title: ConditionNote
ConditionNote.description: Records historical condition notes for an asset, providing an audit trail of its state over time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConditionNote entity.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset this condition note describes. (Relationship: Asset 1:N ConditionNote)

noteDate.description: The date and time when the condition was observed or noted.

conditionDescription.description: A detailed textual description of the asset's condition at the time the note was recorded.

notedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who recorded this condition note. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ConditionNote)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the condition note record was created.

definition.description: Stores user-specific profile information. This is private data, accessible only by the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid` matches `{userId}`). The document's 'firebaseUid' field matches the document ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user, serving as the document ID for their profile.

definition.description: Stores core asset details. Each asset document includes an 'ownerId' (the Firebase UID of the user who created it) and a 'members' map for collaborative access (e.g., { 'uid1': 'editor', 'uid2': 'viewer' }). This denormalization ensures authorization independence and supports secure list operations.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Asset document.

definition.description: Stores metadata for files, photos, and documents associated with a specific asset. Includes denormalized 'assetOwnerId' and 'assetMembers' map from the parent asset for authorization independence, inheriting collaborative permissions.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Asset.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AssetFile document.

definition.description: Records historical condition notes for an asset, providing an audit trail of its state. Includes denormalized 'assetOwnerId' and 'assetMembers' map from the parent asset for authorization independence, inheriting collaborative permissions.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Asset.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ConditionNote document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following strict security and scalability principles, prioritizing Authorization Independence and secure Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs). 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Strategy A):**
This is the most critical aspect of the design. For collaborative data, `Asset` documents include an `ownerId` (referencing the creator's Firebase UID) and a `members` map (e.g., `{"user_uid_1": "editor", "user_uid_2": "viewer"}`). This denormalized authorization context is then *copied* into all documents within its subcollections, specifically `AssetFile` and `ConditionNote` documents. Each `AssetFile` and `ConditionNote` document will contain `assetOwnerId` and `assetMembers` fields, mirroring their parent `Asset`'s authorization data. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules when accessing subcollection data, making rules atomic, simpler to write, and vastly more performant. It also enables atomic creation/deletion operations across parent and child documents within transactions or batched writes.

**Secure Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):**
1.  For user-specific private data like `UserProfile`, the path `/userProfiles/{userId}` uses `request.auth.uid` directly in the rule `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, ensuring that users can only access their own profiles. List operations on `/userProfiles` (though not typical) would still be restricted by `request.auth.uid` matching the document ID.
2.  For collaborative `Asset` data, the `ownerId` and `members` map enable secure `list` operations. Security rules can efficiently filter documents by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` or `resource.data.members[request.auth.uid] != null`. This ensures that a user can only query for assets they own or are a member of, without requiring complex server-side filtering or `get()` calls. This directly supports the 'Collaboration Room' feature where multiple users edit assets in real-time. The same logic applies to `AssetFile` and `ConditionNote` collections, using their denormalized `assetOwnerId` and `assetMembers` fields.

**Structural Segregation (Strategy B):**
Collections are designed to hold documents with homogeneous security requirements. `/userProfiles/{userId}` is strictly for private user data, while `/assets/{assetId}` is for collaboratively managed assets. This clear separation simplifies security rules significantly; there's no need for complex conditional logic to distinguish between public and private data within the same collection. All assets, regardless of `assetType`, share the same collaborative access model, thus residing in a single `/assets` collection.

**Access Modeling (Strategy C):**
*   **Private Data:** `UserProfile` follows path-based ownership (`/userProfiles/{userId}`).
*   **Collaborative Data:** `Asset` uses a `membership map` (`members: {uid: "role"}`) combined with an `ownerId` field. This provides flexible role-based access for multiple users on a per-asset basis.
*   **Hierarchical & Nested Data:** `AssetFile` and `ConditionNote` are subcollections of `Asset`, maintaining logical hierarchy. Their authorization, however, is independent due to denormalization.

**Data Clarity and Predictability (Strategy D):**
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** The `Asset` entity includes a `status` field (e.g., 'Operational', 'Under Repair') for clear state management.
*   **Predictable Schema:** All entities adhere to predefined schemas with static keys. Dynamic keys are avoided.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Standardized field names like `ownerId`, `createdByUserId`, and descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{assetId}`, `{fileId}`, `{noteId}`) are used throughout to improve debuggability and maintainability of rules and application code.
